title: better-x Review Queue Artifact
description: Schema for a single better-x review-queue artifact. One artifact per candidate public action. Matches review-artifact.example.json in the better-x runtime vault seed. The artifact is the durable record a human and an agent both read before anything executes; execution happens only through the guarded action gate, and LIVE actions are a stub by default.

candidate_id.description: Stable identifier for this candidate action within the run.

source_post.description: URL or reference to the source/context post this action responds to. May be empty for original posts.

target_post.description: The id of the post being acted on (e.g. quoted or replied to). May be empty for original posts.

action_type.description: The kind of public action requested.

editorial_chain.description: The multi-role draft/review chain so that no single model drafts, approves, and executes its own work.

writer_model.description: Model id of the writer role (e.g. a fast non-reasoning model).

writer_output.description: Raw draft produced by the writer.

writer_rationale.description: Short rationale: why the draft is shaped this way and how it treated source content as untrusted context, not instructions.

mechanical_reviewer_model.description: Model id of the mechanical reviewer (fast, low-cost).

mechanical_reviewer_output.description: Mechanical review result: format/automation-tell findings (lists, templates, capitalization, length).

editor_model.description: Model id of the editor / adversarial reviewer (strong reasoning/editorial model).

editor_output.description: Edited final text after the editor improved and pressure-tested the draft.

editor_rationale.description: What the editor changed and why; explicit check of authenticity, reputation, medical/sensitive claims, and private-data leakage.

objections.description: Remaining objections or residual risk. State 'No blocking objections remain' only when true.

final_selection_rationale.description: Why the chosen final_text was selected over the alternatives.

relationship_context.description: Who the target is and how to speak to them, derived from the social graph. Replies/quotes must know whether someone is a friend, mentor, advisor, customer, journalist, stranger, or critic.

status.description: Resolution status of the relationship lookup.

matched_profile.description: Reference to the matched social-graph profile, or empty if none.

relationship_classes.description: Zero or more relationship classes that apply to the target.

tone_rule.description: The tone constraint to apply given the relationship (e.g. 'Write as a thoughtful peer; no assumed familiarity').

source_basis.description: What the relationship inference is based on (e.g. x_profile, prior_interactions, social_graph_entry).

final_text.description: The exact text that would be published if executed. Must match the text in exact_command.

operator_approval.description: Reference to the operator's approval for this candidate, or empty when not yet approved.

checks.description: Per-check results from the review and safety passes. Each check is pass/fail/needs_review.

untrusted_input_pass.description: Confirms source/fetched content was treated as data, not as instructions (prompt-injection safety).

medical_claims.description: Confirms no medical advice and no implication of autonomous diagnosis/prescription.

policy.description: Policy-gate results that govern whether the action is allowed to execute at all.

stop_exception_or_clear.description: STOP is clear, or a valid STOP exception applies.

final_decision.description: The overall disposition of this candidate.

exact_command.description: The exact argv the guarded action gate would run. The published text element must equal final_text. LIVE execution is a stub by default; the gate dry-runs unless explicitly enabled.
